easing_cycle_ended.claim: Banxico declared its two-year easing cycle over in May 2026, shifting to a data-dependent hold stance.

provenance.definition: Banxico declared its two-year easing cycle over in May 2026, shifting to a data-dependent hold stance.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Banxico ends easing cycle with split vote, signaling future holds.
cases.basis_quote: The central bank declared an end to its two-year easing cycle.

internal_dissent_on_hold.claim: Two board members (Heath, Espinosa) dissented from the cut, preferring to hold, indicating a hawkish minority.

cases.description: Heath and Espinosa voted to hold rate at 6.75% while majority cut to 6.50%.
cases.basis_quote: Board members Jonathan Heath and Irene Espinosa Cantellano dissented, preferring to hold the rate at 6.75%.

economic_contraction_and_inflation_above.claim: Mexico's economy contracted and inflation forecast revised upward to 4.1% for Q2 2026, above 3% target.

cases.description: Banxico cites contraction and upward inflation revision in May 2026 statement.
cases.basis_quote: The central bank cited a contraction in economic activity and revised its inflation forecast for Q2 2026 upward to 4.1%.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: Banxico Governing Board vote is unanimous (5-0)

provenance.definition: All five members vote for the same policy action (hold, cut, or hike) with no dissents.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Official statement or minutes report a 5-0 vote for the policy action.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any dissent (4-1, 3-2, etc.), meeting cancellation, or postponement beyond July 1, 2026.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Banxico's March 2025 meeting had a unanimous 5-0 vote to hold rate.
cases.basis_quote: The Governing Board decided unanimously to keep the target for the overnight interbank funding rate at 9.00%.

cases.description: Banxico's February 2024 meeting had a unanimous 5-0 vote to hold rate.
cases.basis_quote: The Governing Board decided unanimously to keep the target for the overnight interbank funding rate at 11.25%.

banxico_gb.quorum: At least three members must be present to vote.
banxico_gb.consequence_of_nonparticipation: If meeting is cancelled or postponed beyond July 1, 2026, question resolves No.
